title: Heatwave fuels frustration in Paris's northern suburbs: 'If they'd just given us shutters, we wouldn't be suffering like this'
subhead: Over the past week, daily life in two large concrete housing complexes in Seine-Saint-Denis has changed dramatically due to extreme heat. Residents share their struggles and anger as they try to help each other cope.

title: Jean-Pierre Farandou, French labor minister: 'We cannot bring France to a halt every time it hits 30°C'
Sleeping in a cellar, waiting at a funeral home, riding the tram: How the French are improvising to survive the heatwave
Lebanon, Israel, US sign trilateral framework agreement in Washington
subhead: The agreement comes after five rounds of talks aimed at ending hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon. It includes a pilot effort for Lebanese soldiers to take control of two areas occupied by Israel, as well as a process aimed at disarming Hezbollah.

title: Burkina Faso cuts diplomatic ties with former colonial ruler France
subhead: On Friday, the West African nation's ruling junta announced on national television its plan to sever diplomatic ties with France after accusing the former colonial power of continuing to harbor 'neo-colonial ambitions.' In response, France called it a 'hostile and baseless decision.'

title: Dayot Upamecano, the leader of the French defense, faces the Haaland menace at the 2026 World Cup
subhead: Upamecano, who plays for Bayern Munich, faces the daunting task of limiting Manchester City striker Erling Haaland's influence on Friday, in the high-stakes match between France and Norway.

title: 250 years of American independence: When John Adams predicted that democracy would commit suicide
subhead: 'America 250' (5/13). John Adams, the second president of the United States, and his successor Thomas Jefferson did not share the same view of human nature. Adams, an educated anti-slavery advocate from the North, feared that democracy would perish under the weight of corruption among the powerful. Posterity, however, has largely remembered the soaring rhetoric of Jefferson, the slaveholding Southern planter.

Ukraine's Donbas rear area, from Sloviansk to Kramatorsk, is under constant Russian pressure
subhead: 'Donbas: The War of Attrition' (1/2). Just a few kilometers from one of Ukraine's fiercest fronts, these two cities are under drone strikes that make residents' lives very difficult. Both civilians and soldiers fear the bombings will intensify.
byline: Thomas d’Istria and  Virginie NGuyen Hoang

title: How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
subhead: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.

title: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subhead: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.
